language: Spanish (PCIC baseline: northern-central Peninsular; variety_check cards adjust to the family's country)
source: Plan Curricular del Instituto Cervantes — Niveles de referencia para el español (2006), A1 inventories

cards.title: Greetings & courtesy
cards.goal: Can greet, respond to greetings, and use basic courtesy phrases.
functions: saludar y responder a un saludo
cards.evidence: Three greeting exchanges (meeting, being introduced, leaving) at natural speed, responding appropriately in all three.

cards.title: Introducing yourself
cards.goal: Can introduce him/herself and give basic personal information.
cards.evidence: 30-second self-introduction (name, nationality, city, one extra fact) plus answers to two follow-up questions.

cards.title: Family & having
cards.goal: Can name family members — including in-laws — and say what he/she has.
lexis: family: madre, padre, hermano/a, abuelos, tíos, primos
in-law set (her daily vocabulary): suegro/suegra, cuñado/cuñada, novio
cards.evidence: Walk through the boyfriend's real family naming six people with the correct term and one tener-sentence each.

cards.title: El, la, un, una (gender & articles)
cards.goal: Can use articles and gender/number agreement on nouns.
cards.evidence: For 10 familiar nouns, produce correct article + plural for 8+, then use four in true sentences.
cards.note: Dutch de/het gives the concept but not the agreement consequences — expectation set here, cashed out in ES-A1-05.

cards.title: Describing simply (agreement)
cards.goal: Can describe people and things with basic adjectives, agreeing in gender and number.
cards.evidence: Describe four people/things (mixed gender/number) with two adjectives each; agreement correct in 6/8.

cards.title: Ser vs estar, first pass
cards.goal: Can choose correctly between ser and estar in their core A1 uses.
grammar: ser: identity, origin, traits (es española, es simpática)
estar: location, states (está en Valencia, está cansada)
cards.evidence: Ten gap sentences choosing ser/estar, 8+ correct, then four true sentences (two of each) about real people.
skin_examples: introducing the family to a Dutch friend
cards.note: No Dutch equivalent — concept card. Only the clean A1 split; nuance is B-level.

cards.title: Daily life (present tense)
cards.goal: Can describe daily routines in the present.
lexis: todos los días, por la mañana, normalmente
cards.evidence: Describe a typical day in six sentences including two about another person (3rd person) and one reflexive.

cards.title: Right now (estar + gerundio)
cards.goal: Can say and ask what is happening at the moment.
grammar: estar + gerundio: estoy cocinando, está trabajando
cards.evidence: Answer ¿Qué estás haciendo? truthfully five times across practice moments, and ask it back.

cards.title: Likes & wants
cards.goal: Can express likes, dislikes and wants.
grammar: me gusta / me gustan + the singular/plural trap
cards.evidence: Eight true sentences: three likes, two dislikes (gusta/gustan correct), three wants (2+ with infinitive); respond to two ¿Te gusta…? questions.
cards.note: Gustar is structurally alien to Dutch — fixed pattern first, grammar later.

cards.title: Questions & saying no
cards.goal: Can ask questions with question words and negate correctly.
grammar: interrogativos: qué, quién, dónde, cuándo, cómo, cuánto, por qué
cards.evidence: Question ping-pong: six questions using four different question words, six truthful answers, half negative.
skin_examples: getting to know a family member
quizzing the boyfriend about his childhood

cards.title: Numbers, prices & quantities
cards.goal: Can understand and use numbers, quantities and prices.
cards.evidence: Understand five spoken prices/numbers at natural speed; produce five (ages, prices, phone numbers).

cards.title: Time & days
cards.goal: Can tell the time and talk about days and parts of the day.
grammar: ¿Qué hora es? — Son las… / Es la una
hoy, mañana, ayer, por la mañana/tarde/noche
cards.evidence: Answer five what-time questions and state three true schedule facts with correct time expressions.
cards.note: Teach the clock together with the culture shock: Spanish mealtimes run 1-2 hours later than Belgian ones.

cards.title: At the table
cards.goal: Can navigate the family table: accept, decline politely, ask for things, compliment the food.
lexis: core food and drink as the family cooks it
exponents: No, gracias — está muy rico pero estoy llena
cards.evidence: Full table roleplay: offered five things — accept two, decline three politely, ask for one thing, compliment the dish.
skin_examples: none needed — this is the real world
cards.note: The sobremesa is where family bonding happens; this card is her ticket to it.

cards.title: First plans (ir a + infinitive)
cards.goal: Can say what he/she is going to do.
grammar: el fin de semana, la próxima semana
cards.evidence: Three true going-to sentences about this week plus one ¿Vas a…? question to the listener.
skin_examples: the next visit to the family
cards.note: Future at A1 (analytic, easy), all past tenses at A2 (morphology-heavy) — reverse of the Albanian set, per each grammar's difficulty curve.

cards.title: Connecting ideas
cards.goal: Can link simple sentences with basic connectors.
cards.evidence: Six connected sentences (spoken or short WhatsApp message) with at least two y, two pero, two porque, used correctly.
skin_examples: a voice note about her day
